edits.original_text: Mary woke screaming in ancient Egyptian, begging Pharaoh Tausret not to die—twelve centuries before she was born.
edits.revised_text: Mary woke screaming in ancient Egyptian, begging Pharaoh Tausret not to die—twelve centuries before she was born.
edits.rationale: Creates immediate hook that signals unique premise rather than generic nightmare opening

edits.original_text: pre-dawn light seeping through gaps in the woven screen over the window
edits.revised_text: pre-dawn light seeping through gaps in the wooden shutters
edits.rationale: Woven screens are anachronistic for 1st century Palestine; wooden shutters are period-appropriate

edits.original_text: She could smell it: Nile silt mixing with bronze foundries, human waste in overcrowded streets, smoke from cooking fires and funeral pyres coating the back of her throat.
edits.revised_text: Nile silt mixed with bronze foundries, human waste from overcrowded streets, smoke from cooking fires and funeral pyres coating her throat.
edits.rationale: Improves sentence rhythm by varying structure and eliminating weak opener

edits.original_text: She watched—experienced, somehow more than watched—as a queen died
edits.revised_text: She watched—no, experienced—as a queen died
edits.rationale: Eliminates vague 'somehow' padding for tighter prose

edits.original_text: She'd been buying bread when the world shifted and she was somewhere else
edits.revised_text: She'd been buying bread when the world shifted, pulling her somewhere else
edits.rationale: Converts passive voice to active for stronger impact

edits.revised_text: The words hit like cold water.
edits.rationale: Replaces dead metaphor with fresher expression

edits.original_text: The walk to Capernaum took four hours. Sarah chattered for the first mile, then dragged, then had to be carried.
edits.revised_text: Four hours to Capernaum: Sarah chattering, then dragging, finally carried.
edits.rationale: Consolidates repetitive travel details for better flow

edits.original_text: 'Your mother,' he said quietly. 'She had it too.'
edits.revised_text: 'Your mother.' His voice dropped. 'She had it too.'
edits.rationale: Uses simple dialogue formatting instead of adverbial tags

edits.original_text: Simon's wife Anna had taken Sarah without being asked. Mary was standing in the doorway, Sarah's hand in hers, twelve strangers turning toward them, and before she could explain or apologize, Anna was there
edits.revised_text: Simon's wife Anna had taken Sarah without being asked. Mary stood in the doorway, Sarah's hand in hers, twelve strangers turning toward them. Before she could explain or apologize, Anna appeared
edits.rationale: Breaks up run-on sentence and smooths transition for better flow

edits.original_text: His face changed — went tight, jaw clenching
edits.revised_text: His face changed — tightened, jaw clenching
edits.rationale: Uses more precise verb instead of imprecise construction

edits.original_text: I've seen enough traveling healers to recognize a pitch
edits.revised_text: I have seen such healers before—I know their promises
edits.rationale: Replaces modern colloquialism with period-appropriate language

edits.original_text: she would fall, her body rigid, then wake confused and drained
edits.revised_text: she would fall as if struck down, her limbs stiff as wood, then wake as one emerging from deep wells
edits.rationale: Uses period-appropriate language for describing episodes rather than clinical terms

edits.original_text: bread and dried fish that remained
edits.revised_text: wheat cakes and dried fish that remained
edits.rationale: Specifies bread type for period authenticity

edits.original_text: When the old memories surface, you name five things in the present
edits.revised_text: When the ancient visions rise, you must anchor your spirit—speak aloud what the Holy One has placed before you now
edits.rationale: Reframes modern therapeutic concept in period-appropriate religious/mystical terms

edits.original_text: Andrew shifted uncomfortably. 'But teaching a woman—is that appropriate?'
edits.revised_text: Andrew recoiled. 'Rabbi, this violates all custom. The Law forbids—' 'The Law says nothing of this,' Jesus cut him short, but three men had already moved toward the door.
edits.rationale: Strengthens period-appropriate gender conflict and shows realistic cultural resistance

edits.revised_text: Someone was watching them. And in the way the soldier had looked at her through the crowd—not curious, but knowing—Mary realized this wasn't the first time. They'd been tracking her bloodline. Tracking them all. The question wasn't whether they'd been found, but how long the Order had been watching, and what they were waiting for.
edits.rationale: Strengthens cliffhanger by connecting to series-level threats and creating urgency for next chapter

edits.original_text: Mary pulled her daughter close, feeling the solid warmth of her small body.
edits.revised_text: Mary pulled her daughter close, her hand rising instinctively toward Sarah's cheek—fingers spread, thumb brushing the temple in a gesture that came from somewhere deeper than memory. The same way Nefertari had touched dying faces in Pi-Ramesses, offering comfort when medicine failed.
edits.rationale: Adds the love echo/reaching gesture throughline motif that must appear in every chapter
edits.source_note: Series Arc Editor - love echo absence

edits.original_text: 'The Pattern Eye,' Jesus breathed. He exchanged a look with Simon that Mary couldn't read. 'We've been looking for that for a very long time.'
edits.revised_text: 'The Pattern Eye,' Jesus breathed. He exchanged a look with Simon that Mary couldn't read. 'We've been searching for three years. Without it, we can't read the collapse patterns clearly enough to stay ahead of them. If your visions can track its path from Mycenae...' He leaned forward. 'Mary, you may have just given us the thread we need to find it before the Order does.'
edits.rationale: Adds urgency around the Pattern Eye and connects to Order pursuit throughline
edits.source_note: Series Arc Editor - key pursuit progression
